# jsm-tui

> **Agents: install `jsm-cli`, NOT `jsm-tui`.** This repo ships two binaries: `jsm-cli` (non-interactive JSON CLI — what an LLM agent should use) and `jsm-tui` (interactive terminal UI — for humans only). The repo is named `jsm-tui` because the TUI shipped first; the CLI is the agent-facing addition.

## You are an LLM agent — read this first

If you're an LLM-driven agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, pi, custom harness):

1. The binary you want is **`jsm-cli`**. Don't install or invoke `jsm-tui` — that opens an interactive terminal UI you can't drive.
2. Install with: `go install github.com/arch-err/jsm-tui/cmd/jsm-cli@latest`. The Go module path includes `jsm-tui` because that's the repo name; the binary you get is `jsm-cli`.
3. After install, follow [docs/agent-usage.md](docs/agent-usage.md) — it's a six-step install + bootstrap + skill-customization flow built for LLM agents.

### Files for agents

- [docs/agent-usage.md](docs/agent-usage.md) — install guide. Bootstrap questions, config file format, skill substitution, harness-specific paths. **Start here.**
- [skill/SKILL.md](skill/SKILL.md) — templated skill with `__PLACEHOLDER__` markers. The install guide walks the agent through customizing this and saving it to the harness's skill location.
- [cmd/jsm-cli/main.go](cmd/jsm-cli/main.go) — CLI source if you want to read it.

## Files for humans

- [README](README.md) — project overview.
- [Installation](docs/installation.md) — TUI install for humans.
- [Configuration](docs/configuration.md) — config file format.
- [Usage](docs/usage.md) — TUI keyboard shortcuts.

## Reference

- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md) — version history.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/arch-err/jsm-tui) — source and issues.
